India is monitoring the situation as the blockade begins.

story_clusters.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman criticized Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin over claims regarding a central advisory on paddy and wheat bonuses ahead of the Tamil Nadu polls. Multiple sources report that Sitharaman accused Stalin of spreading a "false narrative" about the Centre's stance on farm bonuses. The dispute centers on Tamil Nadu's demand for a higher paddy bonus and the Centre's alleged advisory against it.

story_clusters.summary: The U.S. is planning to blockade ships entering and exiting Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz following stalled talks in Islamabad, which has caused oil prices to rise above $100. Author Ray Locker warns this move could escalate tensions with China and disrupt global shipping. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for global oil trade, and the blockade is expected to significantly impact the global economy.

Separately, the NCLT Allahabad has ruled that homebuyers cannot retain recovery amounts due to priority rules under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), as stated by TaxGuru. perspectives.title: Southwest monsoon season rainfall to be below normal, says IMD

story_clusters.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts that the southwest monsoon rainfall for 2024 will be below normal, at 92% of the Long Period Average (LPA). This prediction is attributed to the influence of El Niño conditions, which are expected to weaken the monsoon. The IMD has indicated that the monsoon will be 8% below the LPA.

articles.title: What Happens to Old Tax Laws Under Income Tax Act, 2025? Section 536 Explained
articles.summary: Section 536 of the Income Tax Act, 2025 ensures that old tax laws under the 1961 Act remain applicable for refunds, losses, credits, and pending proceedings. This provision prevents disruption caused by the repeal of the earlier law, maintaining existing rights and liabilities. The explanation clarifies how the transition to the new tax regime preserves continuity for taxpayers and authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The provision safeguards refunds, losses, credits, and pending proceedings under the old law. It prevents disruption caused by repeal of the 1961 Act. articles.title: CBDT Circular 4/2026 & DIN Defects: Scope of Section 292BA Explained
articles.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) issued Circular 4/2026, which introduces flexibility in quoting the Document Identification Number (DIN) for tax-related references. However, it does not address cases where DIN is completely absent or where jurisdictional defects exist. The circular clarifies the scope of Section 292BA, which governs procedural compliance in tax matters. This impacts taxpayers and tax authorities by setting guidelines for DIN-related compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The new circular allows flexible DIN referencing instead of strict quoting. However, it does not validate cases with complete absence of DIN or jurisdictional defects. The post CBDT Circular 4/2026   DIN Defects: Scope of Section 292BA Explained appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Madras HC orders independent probe into illegal panchayat ostracism depriving woman’s family of basic civic rights
articles.summary: The Madras High Court has ordered an independent investigation into the illegal ostracism of a woman’s family by a panchayat, which deprived them of basic civic rights. The court directed respondents to ensure no injustice is caused to the petitioner, citing violations of her fundamental rights under Article 21 of the Constitution. The ruling highlights the need to protect vulnerable groups from extra-legal actions by local bodies.
articles.raw_summary: The respondents are directed to conduct enquiry independently in this regard and ensure that no injustice is caused to the petitioner by violating her fundamental rights as contemplated under Article 21 of the Constitution . articles.title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official
articles.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement is set to begin in the second week of May. This pact will allow 99 percent of Indian exports to enter Britain duty-free. Tariffs on British products like cars and whisky will also be reduced in India. A separate agreement will prevent double social levies for temporary workers. This aims to boost trade significantly.

articles.title: UP approves revised minimum wages across categories after Noida workers unrest
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government has approved revised minimum wages across categories after protests by workers in Noida. The new interim rates, effective from April 1, will increase pay for unskilled, semi-skilled, and skilled workers across different regions. This decision follows unrest and aims to address worker grievances. The state government's approval marks a policy response to the protests.

This resolves confusion and allows delayed filings to be regularized if reasonable cause is shown. The decision ensures proper authority allocation for tax compliance matters.

This ensures that delayed Form 10A filings can be regularized upon showing reasonable cause. articles.title: DRP 130426: CAG Reveals Governance Failure in J&K Wetlands Management
articles.summary: A CAG report reveals that the Jammu and Kashmir government's conservation efforts for wetlands were limited to just six lakes, while 691 other lakes were ignored. The Forest Department did not identify eligible water bodies or seek assistance under central schemes. This raises concerns about governance and environmental policy implementation in the region.
articles.raw_summary: According to the report, government conservation efforts were limited to only six lakes—Dal, Wular, Hokersar, Manasbal, Surinsar, and Mansar—which were examined in detail. For the remaining 691 lakes, the Forest Department neither identified eligible water bodies nor formulated plans to seek assistance under schemes of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF CC).

articles.title: Income Tax Assessment Quashed Due to Invalid Notice by Officer Lacking Pecuniary Jurisdiction
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed an income tax assessment after ruling that the initial notice issued to the taxpayer was invalid because the officer lacked the necessary pecuniary jurisdiction. The tribunal held that jurisdictional defects can be raised at any stage of proceedings. This decision underscores the importance of proper legal authority in tax assessments and may influence future tax administration practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal admitted a new legal ground and held that jurisdictional defects can be raised at any stage. It quashed the assessment as the initial notice itself was issued without authority. articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant stated at an arbitration conference that the term 'Alternative Dispute Resolution' may need to be reconsidered, as arbitration is no longer an alternative but a primary method of resolving disputes. The remarks were made during a discussion on the evolving landscape of dispute resolution in India.

articles.title: Section 68 Not Applicable to Genuine Sales Transactions Supported by Documentation
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that documented sales transactions recorded in books cannot be treated as unexplained cash credits under Section 68 of the tax law. The decision clarifies that genuine sales supported by proper documentation are not subject to taxation under this provision. This ruling impacts how businesses document and report sales for tax purposes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal examined whether sales receipts can be treated as unexplained cash credits. It held that documented sales recorded in books cannot be taxed under Section 68. articles.title: For Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma's Recusal, Kejriwal Relies On Court Accepting ED Plea To Change Judge In Satyender Jain's Case
articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal has cited a reasonable apprehension of bias against Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma, arguing that she tends to accept the ED's submissions without scrutiny. Kejriwal's request for her recusal is tied to the court's acceptance of the Enforcement Directorate's plea to change the judge in Satyender Jain's case. The matter highlights concerns about judicial impartiality and the process of judge assignment in high-profile cases.
articles.raw_summary: 'I have reasonable apprehension of bias," Kejriwal said, contending that Justice Sharma tends to accept whatever ED & CBI said.

articles.title: The Concept of Regularisation in Service Jurisprudence: Before and After Umadevi: The Constitutional Transformation of Regularisation in Public Employment
articles.summary: The article examines the evolution of regularisation in public employment in India, focusing on the impact of the Supreme Court's judgment in Umadevi (2006). It analyzes how this ruling transformed constitutional principles governing government appointments and service conditions. The discussion highlights legal and policy implications for public sector hiring and workforce management. The analysis is authored by Shamik Chatterjee and published on SCC Online Blog.
articles.raw_summary: by Shamik Chatterjee* The post The Concept of Regularisation in Service Jurisprudence: Before and After Umadevi: The Constitutional Transformation of Regularisation in Public Employment appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: How the Iran War Will Upend the Global Economy
articles.summary: A potential war involving Iran could disrupt global energy supplies, triggering an energy shock and exacerbating debt crises worldwide. The conflict may impact oil prices and financial markets, posing risks to economies dependent on energy imports. The article highlights concerns over supply chain disruptions and economic instability, which could have far-reaching consequences for countries like India.
articles.raw_summary: The Iran war risks not just an energy Shock—but also a debt crisis.

articles.title: CESTAT Allowed Home Consumption for Confiscated Goods Due to Beneficial Notification Amendment
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that goods confiscated as prohibited can now be cleared for domestic use if a beneficial policy amendment is applied retrospectively. The tribunal held that such amendments apply retroactively, permitting home consumption after payment of applicable duties. The decision impacts customs and tax regulations for previously confiscated goods.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal examined whether goods confiscated as prohibited could be cleared domestically after a policy change. It held that beneficial amendments apply retrospectively, allowing home consumption upon payment of duty. The post CESTAT Allowed Home Consumption for Confiscated Goods Due to Beneficial Notification Amendment appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CIRP Admitted Due to Established Loan Default Despite Pending Arbitration Challenge
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that a loan default can trigger insolvency proceedings under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) even if the borrower has challenged the default in arbitration. The tribunal held that the default exists independently of the arbitration challenge and that the absence of a stay on the arbitral award allows the initiation of CIRP. This decision impacts lenders and borrowers navigating debt recovery and insolvency processes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that default under the loan agreement existed independently of the arbitral award challenge. It ruled that absence of a stay on the award allows initiation of insolvency proceedings. The post CIRP Admitted Due to Established Loan Default Despite Pending Arbitration Challenge appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Kerala HC allows two transgender persons to continue hormone therapy amid petitions against new law
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court permitted two transgender individuals to continue hormone therapy, stating that abruptly stopping such treatment could lead to 'absurd results.' The court's decision comes amid petitions challenging a new law related to transgender rights. This ruling underscores the judiciary's role in protecting vulnerable groups' access to essential healthcare. The case highlights ongoing legal and policy debates around transgender rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court said that if such treatment is brought to an abrupt stop for a person, there could be very ‘absurd results’ for them.

The framework aims to simplify processes for companies while maintaining transparency and adherence to disclosure norms.

articles.title: SC Dismissed Insolvency Plea as Prior Dispute Established Through Correspondence & Records
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed an insolvency plea after establishing that prior disputes over defective goods and account reconciliation existed before a demand notice was issued. The Court ruled that such pre-existing disputes prevent the admission of insolvency proceedings under Section 9 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). This decision clarifies the legal threshold for initiating insolvency cases when prior commercial disagreements are involved.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that ongoing disputes regarding defective goods and account reconciliation existed prior to the demand notice. It ruled that such disputes bar admission of insolvency proceedings under Section 9 of the IBC. articles.title: The rise of infrastructure for digital censorshipA series of IT Rules amendments has shifted online speech control from courts to the executive, enabling fast, opaque takedowns with limited accountability.12 April, 20266 min readApar GuptaNikhil Pahwa
articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

articles.title: SC: Partnership Cannot Be Used to Mask Illegal Sub-Lease; Courts Will Lift the Veil
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a partnership cannot be used to hide illegal sub-letting of property. The court held that if possession is effectively transferred to third parties, judges can disregard the partnership structure and treat the arrangement as sub-letting. This decision prioritizes the real nature of transactions over their legal form in tenancy disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether a partnership can shield unlawful sub-letting. The Supreme Court held that where possession is effectively transferred to third parties, courts can lift the veil and treat it as sub-letting. The ruling reinforces that substance prevails over form in tenancy disputes. The post SC: Partnership Cannot Be Used to Mask Illegal Sub-Lease; Courts Will Lift the Veil appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Form MSME-1 Filing What Every Company Must Know
articles.summary: The article clarifies that companies must file Form MSME-1 if payments to MSME suppliers exceed the 45-day limit, even for a single delayed payment. It highlights that outstanding dues and reasons for delay must be disclosed. This requirement stems from regulatory compliance under Indian law, impacting corporate governance and MSME sector transparency.
articles.raw_summary: The case clarifies that MSME-1 filing is triggered when payments to MSME suppliers exceed the 45-day limit. It emphasizes that even a single delay requires disclosure of outstanding dues and reasons. The post Form MSME-1 Filing What Every Company Must Know appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Grassroots-level SIR drives impacted voter turnout in Kerala, say community forums
articles.summary: Community forums in Kerala reported that grassroots-level voter awareness drives by volunteers helped improve voter turnout by guiding people through documentation procedures. The efforts were driven by widespread concerns over potential disenfranchisement. Volunteers played a key role in addressing procedural barriers to voting.
articles.raw_summary: Volunteers had guided voters through documentation procedures amid widespread concern over disenfranchisement

articles.title: 'Will be gross injustice if delayed': PM Modi urges support for women’s reservation bill ahead of Parliament session
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has addressed the women of India, emphasizing "Nari Shakti" and the government's commitment to women's reservation in legislative bodies. He highlighted the upcoming special Parliament session to pass a constitutional amendment for the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, aiming for its full implementation by the 2029 elections.
articles.raw_summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has addressed the women of India, emphasizing "Nari Shakti" and the government's commitment to women's reservation in legislative bodies. He highlighted the upcoming special Parliament session to pass a constitutional amendment for the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, aiming for its full implementation by the 2029 elections.

articles.title: From Kovalam to Kokilamedu, livelihoods of hundreds threatened by proposed Mamallan reservoir
articles.summary: A proposed dam project, the Mamallan reservoir, threatens the livelihoods of hundreds of fisherfolk in villages from Kovalam to Kokilamedu in Tamil Nadu. Citizen Matters reports that local communities are concerned about the project's impact on their way of life. The dam, if built, could disrupt fishing grounds and coastal ecosystems. Activists and affected residents may push for policy interventions to address these concerns.
articles.raw_summary: Citizen Matters travelled from Kovalam to Mahabalipuram to talk to fisher communities about the upcoming Mamallan reservoir dam project The post From Kovalam to Kokilamedu, livelihoods of hundreds threatened by proposed Mamallan reservoir appeared first on Citizen Matters .

articles.title: ITAT Mumbai: No Section 68 Addition Merely Because Shareholder Company Struck Off by ROC
articles.summary: The case examined whether old share capital can be taxed due to ROC strike-off of a shareholder. ITAT held that Section 68 applies only to credits in the relevant year. The ruling clarifies that historical transactions remain valid. The post ITAT Mumbai: No Section 68 Addition Merely Because Shareholder Company Struck Off by ROC appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether old share capital can be taxed due to ROC strike-off of a shareholder. ITAT held that Section 68 applies only to credits in the relevant year. articles.title: SC notice to Centre, others on plea concerning MSP
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, led by Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi, has issued notices to the Centre, the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices, and others. The notices seek their response on a plea concerning the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for agricultural produce. The court's intervention highlights a legal challenge to existing agricultural policy frameworks. This development could impact government decisions on MSP and related agricultural policies.

articles.title: Plea Filed In Supreme Court Against S.44(3) Of DPDP Act, Seeks Interim Relief Against Masking/Deletion Of Available Data; Notice Issued
articles.summary: A public interest litigation has been filed in the Supreme Court against Section 44(3) of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP), which replaces a provision in the Right to Information (RTI) Act. The petitioners seek interim relief to prevent the masking or deletion of data already available under RTI or other welfare laws, arguing it would undermine transparency. The Supreme Court has issued a notice in response to the plea.
articles.raw_summary: A public interest litigation has been filed before the Supreme Court against Section 44(3) of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, which substituted Section 8(1)(j) of the Right to Information Act and exempted disclosure of any sort of "personal information" of a person. The petitioners have also sought an interim relief against deletion/masking of data already made available on public portals in compliance of provisions of the RTI Act or other welfare legislations, as it would impair groun

articles.title: IT rules have made the internet less freeIt is unfortunate that demands for accountability have been repeatedly deflected and changes to the IT Rules have been framed as a response to deepfakes and AI-generated misinformation.06 April, 20262 min readApar Gupta
articles.summary: The article criticizes India's IT Rules, arguing they have reduced internet freedom. It highlights that demands for accountability have been deflected and changes to the rules are framed as a response to deepfakes and AI-generated misinformation. The piece is authored by Apar Gupta and published by the Internet Freedom Foundation on April 6, 2026.

articles.title: Fact-check: MP Nishikant Dubey’s claim on X community notes & Australian tax is false
articles.summary: MP Nishikant Dubey claimed that a parliamentary committee had recommended treating X (formerly Twitter) as a publisher to the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). However, MediaNama's fact-check found no such recommendation in the committee's report. The claim highlights ongoing discussions about regulating social media platforms in India, particularly regarding their legal status and accountability under Indian law.
articles.raw_summary: Dubey claims that the Parliament's Standing Committee on Communications and IT had recommended to MeitY that X get treated as a publisher, but the committee's report doesn't mention it. The post Fact-check: MP Nishikant Dubey s claim on X community notes Australian tax is false appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

articles.title: #DataViz: How Women’s Representation Declines From Panchayats To Parliament
articles.summary: Reservation policies have increased women's participation in local governance bodies like Panchayats, but their representation remains significantly lower in Parliament and higher offices. This disparity underscores ongoing challenges in achieving gender parity in political representation. The article highlights the need for further policy measures to address this gap.
articles.raw_summary: Reservation has increased women’s presence in local bodies, but representation remains significantly lower in Parliament and higher offices

articles.title: Dispute between dermatologists, dentists takes a legal turn in Kerala
articles.summary: A dispute has arisen in Kerala between dermatologists and dentists over who is legally authorized to perform hair transplantation and cosmetic surgeries. The disagreement highlights regulatory gaps in medical practice standards and could prompt government or professional body intervention to clarify scope of practice rules. The Hindu National reports the conflict has taken a legal turn, raising questions about healthcare policy and professional licensing in India.
articles.raw_summary: The disagreement is over who is authorised to perform hair transplantation and aesthetic and cosmetic surgeries

articles.title: Punjab Cabinet clears bill proposing life term for ‘sacrilege’ of Guru Granth Sahib
articles.summary: The Punjab Cabinet has approved a draft amendment to state law proposing life imprisonment for the sacrilege of the Guru Granth Sahib. The move follows multiple incidents that the government states have deeply hurt public sentiments and caused unrest. The bill will now proceed through the legislative process. The amendment reflects the state's response to religious sensitivities and public order concerns.
articles.raw_summary: The draft amendment to the law follows several incidents that have ‘deeply hurt public sentiments’ and caused unrest, the government said.

articles.title: Orphaned by the pandemic, let down by government
articles.summary: Six years after the COVID-19 pandemic, children who lost parents to the virus continue to struggle with education and financial support. Government schemes at both central and state levels have failed to provide adequate assistance, leaving these children in distress. The article highlights systemic gaps in policy execution and the urgent need for intervention to address their welfare and educational rights.
articles.raw_summary: Six years after Covid-19 epidemic, several children continue to struggle for education and money as the implementation of state and central schemes falters.

articles.title: Assessment Order Set Aside for Ignoring Valid Revised Return Filed Within Time
articles.summary: The court set aside an assessment order after finding that tax authorities ignored a valid revised return filed within the stipulated time. The ruling clarified that a revised return supersedes the original return, and ignoring it constitutes a legal error. The case has been remanded for fresh assessment. The decision impacts tax compliance and administrative procedures for taxpayers and revenue authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that once a revised return is filed, the original return stands obliterated. Ignoring the revised return was treated as a legal error, leading to remand for fresh assessment. The post Assessment Order Set Aside for Ignoring Valid Revised Return Filed Within Time appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Kerala dental student’s death highlights growing threat of predatory loan apps
articles.summary: A Kerala dental student died by suicide after taking a ₹14,000 loan from a predatory loan app. Recovery agents repeatedly contacted him and allegedly harassed a college teacher to shame him into repayment. The incident underscores the rising threat of exploitative digital lending practices in India. Activists and authorities may push for stricter regulations on fintech lending apps.
articles.raw_summary: Investigators say the student had taken a loan of around ₹14,000 from an app and recovery agents had been contacting him repeatedly. They also purportedly directed calls and messages to a college teacher in an attempt to shame the youngster and coerce repayment

articles.title: Pigmy agents are employees of Bank and not business facilitators hence no GST leviable on commission
articles.summary: Karnataka High Court held that pigmy agents employed by the Bank can never be treated as business facilitators and qualifies as employees of the Bank and hence commission paid to pigmy agents is clearly exempt from levy of GST in terms of Sl.No.1 of Schedule III. articles.summary: The Left Democratic Front (LDF) in Kerala announced candidate lists for the 2026 Assembly elections under the 'Nava Keralam' banner. The Communist Party of India (Marxist) declared 86 candidates, including incumbent Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, while the Communist Party of India listed 25. Polling is scheduled for April 9, with vote counting on May 4. The election results could impact future state policies and governance in Kerala.

articles.title: Addition u/s 68 was unsustainable without rebuttal of NBFC genuineness or tracing money trail
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that an addition under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act could not be sustained against an assessee if the assessee proves the genuineness of an NBFC investor and the assessing officer (AO) fails to rebut this evidence or trace the money trail. The ruling highlights the burden on tax authorities to establish fraud or lack of credibility in such cases, reinforcing legal safeguards for taxpayers in disputes involving investments from NBFCs.
articles.raw_summary: Addition under Section 68 could not be sustained where assessee has established the genuineness of a Non-banking financial company (NBFC) investor, and the AO failed to rebut such evidence or trace any money trail linking the assessee to the invested funds. This decision impacts how tax authorities interpret and apply road tax rules in such cases.

articles.title: So, Here's the Problem: Textile Factories in 10 Nations Face Inconsistent Environmental Permit Rules, UN Study Finds
articles.summary: A UNEP report found that environmental permitting systems in ten major textile-producing nations, including India, have significant gaps in regulating industrial pollution from textile factories. Most national laws do not classify textile activities as environmentally significant or consider gendered impacts on workers. The study highlights inconsistencies in how spinning mills, dye houses, and garment factories are regulated, raising concerns about worker safety and environmental compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Environmental permitting systems across ten major textile-producing nations contain significant gaps in how industrial pollution from spinning mills, dye houses and garment factories is regulated, a new UNEP report has found. Most national legislation fails to classify textile activities as environmentally significant or to consider gendered impacts on workers.

articles.title: Homebuyer Cannot Retain Recovery Amount Due to Priority Rules Under IBC: NCLT Allahabad
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) in Allahabad ruled that a homebuyer cannot retain recovery amounts from an insolvency estate due to priority rules under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The tribunal directed the refund of the amount to the insolvency estate and limited the homebuyer's claim to the admitted amount, citing that allowing retention would constitute preferential recovery. The ruling impacts how homebuyers recover dues during insolvency proceedings under the IBC.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that allowing retention of the amount would amount to preferential recovery. It directed refund to the insolvency estate and limited the claim to the admitted amount. articles.title: Chevron, Shell to sign agreements for oil, gas areas in Venezuela, sources say
articles.summary: Chevron and Shell are set to sign agreements to expand oil and gas operations in Venezuela. Chevron will return a gas field and expand in the Orinoco heavy crude belt, while Shell will operate the Loran gas field. These deals follow Venezuela's recent hydrocarbon law reforms and easing of sanctions to attract foreign investment. The developments may influence global energy markets and India's energy import strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Chevron is set to sign agreements to return a gas field in Venezuela and expand operations in the Orinoco heavy crude belt, while Shell prepares to operate the Loran gas field. The deals reflect Venezuela’s push to attract foreign energy investment following major hydrocarbon law reforms and sanctions easing.

articles.title: ITAT Pune: Deemed Dividend Upheld, But AO Directed to Recompute After Interest Adjustment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune upheld the taxability of deemed dividends under Section 2(22)(e) of the Income Tax Act, even for temporary shareholder withdrawals, stating that repayment duration does not affect tax liability. The Assessing Officer (AO) was directed to recompute the tax after adjusting for interest. The ruling impacts how companies and shareholders report and pay taxes on such transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that even temporary withdrawals by a shareholder can trigger deemed dividend under Section 2(22)(e). It held that duration or repayment does not negate taxability. The post ITAT Pune: Deemed Dividend Upheld, But AO Directed to Recompute After Interest Adjustment appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that religious bodies can claim compensation for the death of their head in a road accident, even if the deceased was not a family member. The court held that dependency under motor accident laws is not limited to family members, allowing institutions to claim compensation for institutional loss. This decision broadens the interpretation of dependency in such cases.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that dependency under motor accident law is not confined to family members. Religious institutions relying on the deceased can claim compensation for institutional loss. articles.title: Taxability of Keyman Insurance Policy Under Income-Tax Act, 1961
articles.summary: The article explains the tax implications of Keyman Insurance Policies under India's Income-Tax Act, 1961. It notes that while premiums paid by businesses are deductible as expenses, the proceeds from such policies are fully taxable. Additionally, assigning the policy may trigger salary taxation, highlighting the need for careful tax planning. The analysis is based on provisions of the Income-Tax Act.
articles.raw_summary: Premiums are deductible as business expenses, but proceeds are fully taxable. Assignment may trigger salary taxation, making careful planning essential. The post Taxability of Keyman Insurance Policy Under Income-Tax Act, 1961 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: What are Karnataka’s AI-focussed responsible social media, digital safety rules?
articles.summary: Karnataka is proposing a new digital safety bill to regulate social media platforms. The bill aims to use AI for content moderation, require labels for synthetic content, and enforce faster removal of harmful posts. It prioritizes user safety, especially for younger users, and includes stricter timelines and institutional oversight for compliance. The bill reflects broader efforts to address digital risks in India's growing online ecosystem.
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka’s proposed digital safety bill seeks to tighten social media regulation with AI-led moderation, mandatory labelling of synthetic content, and quicker action on harmful posts. User safety, especially for younger audiences, stricter timelines and institutional oversight to enforce compliance are among its other aims.

This initiative aims to strengthen India's EV value chain and reduce dependency on imports.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune ruled that no penalty can be imposed on taxpayers if an incorrect tax return was due to a mistake by their tax consultant. This decision sets a precedent for tax compliance and penalty assessments in cases involving professional misconduct.

articles.title: Home ministry proposes to increase CISF quota for ex-Agniveers to 50%
articles.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has proposed increasing the reservation quota for ex-Agniveers in the Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) from 10% to 50%. This proposal exempts them from written exams and physical tests. The move aims to provide better employment opportunities for ex-Agniveers, who are veterans of the Agnipath Scheme. The decision reflects a policy shift to support demobilized military personnel in securing government jobs.
articles.raw_summary: The MHA proposes increasing ex-Agniveers' CISF recruitment reservation from 10% to 50%, exempting them from exams and physical tests.

articles.title: After 7 years, work restarts on India-funded Bhutan hydro project
articles.summary: India is funding the project, which is expected to be completed within five years and will export surplus power to India.
articles.raw_summary: India is funding the project, which is expected to be completed within five years and will export surplus power to India.

articles.title: Nvidia rewards 10k India staff, payouts up to 1 crore
articles.summary: Nvidia's India employees are receiving a special stock grant, the 'Jensen Special Grant,' adding 25% to their initial RSUs. Payouts range from over Rs 5 lakh to Rs 1 crore, vesting over four years. This initiative highlights the growing importance of equity-led compensation in attracting and retaining talent in India's booming semiconductor and AI sectors.

Payouts range from over Rs 5 lakh to Rs 1 crore, vesting over four years. This initiative highlights the growing importance of equity-led compensation in attracting and retaining talent in India's booming semiconductor and AI sectors.

articles.title: Owaisi condemns ‘exploitation’ of Muslim vote at campaign rally
articles.summary: AIM leader Owaisi criticized Mamata Banerjee and Kabir Sumon for allegedly using Muslim voters in West Bengal as a 'vote bank' and committing 'grave injustice' against them. He raised concerns about the exploitation of the Muslim community's political support during a campaign rally. The remarks highlight tensions over minority rights and electoral practices in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Sharpens attack on both Mamata and Kabir for ‘grave injustice’ towards Muslims of Bengal and using them as a vote bank

articles.title: Blockchain For Impact launches $50-m innovation platform for medtech ecosystem
articles.summary: Blockchain For Impact, a private initiative, has launched a $50 million platform to support biomedical and medtech innovations in India. The platform aims to bridge the gap between research and deployable healthcare products. This effort could help accelerate healthcare advancements but does not involve direct government policy action.
articles.raw_summary: The platform aims to help biomedical and medtech innovation move from research to deployable products in the healthcare system

articles.title: Days after searches, ED arrests I-PAC co-founder & director Vinesh Chandel
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has arrested Vinesh Chandel, a director and co-founder of political consultancy firm I-PAC, in connection with a money-laundering probe linked to an alleged coal scam in West Bengal. The agency is investigating past payments to I-PAC, with dealings involving former AAP communication advisor Vijay Nair also under scrutiny.
articles.raw_summary: The Enforcement Directorate has arrested Vinesh Chandel, a director and co-founder of political consultancy firm I-PAC, in connection with a money-laundering probe linked to an alleged coal scam in West Bengal. The agency is investigating past payments to I-PAC, with dealings involving former AAP communication advisor Vijay Nair also under scrutiny.

articles.title: SC seeks Centre’s response on plea over MSP linked to cost of cultivation
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is examining a petition that seeks to link Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for crops to the actual cost of cultivation, as determined by state-led assessments. The plea highlights the financial struggles of farmers who often sell produce below production costs. A ruling in favor of the petition could reshape agricultural policies and improve farmer livelihoods across India. The Centre has been asked to respond to the appeal.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court is currently reviewing an appeal aimed at elevating the significance of state-led assessments of farming costs in the establishment of minimum support prices. As farmers struggle with financial difficulties—often selling their produce for less than it costs to grow—this examination has the potential to transform agricultural frameworks and improve livelihood conditions for farmers nationwide.

articles.summary: The Vande Bharat Express, an indigenously developed semi-high-speed train service by the Indian Railways, saw a 34% year-on-year increase in occupancy in FY26. Since its launch, the train has been a key part of India's railway modernization efforts. The growth in ridership reflects its increasing popularity among passengers. The data may inform future policy decisions on railway infrastructure and service expansion.

articles.summary: The CPI-M (Communist Party of India - Marxist) in Kerala has formed a tactical alliance with the SDPI (Social Democratic Party of India), the political wing of the banned PFI (Popular Front of India). The SDPI aims to replicate the role of AIMIM (All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen) to weaken the UDF (United Democratic Front) in the state. articles.title: Supreme Court asks states to treat rising child trafficking cases as grave; seeks coordinated action
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has warned states about the rise in child trafficking cases and criticized their lack of action in enforcing a 2025 anti-trafficking ruling. Many states have not formed required review panels. The court has directed coordinated action and scheduled the next hearing for April 29. This follows concerns over child rights violations and the need for stricter enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court warns of rising child trafficking, slams states for laxity in enforcing 2025 anti trafficking ruling, many have not formed review panels, next hearing on April 29.

articles.title: From Maoist stronghold to tap water: Remote Abujhmad village gets first home supply
articles.summary: A remote village in Abujhmad, previously a Maoist stronghold, has received its first home tap water supply under a government initiative. This marks a significant development in providing basic amenities to a historically underserved and conflict-affected region. The project highlights efforts to improve rural infrastructure and welfare in tribal areas.

articles.title: RS to elect its deputy chairman on Apr 17; NDA likely to renominate Harivansh
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha will elect its Deputy Chairman on April 17, with the NDA likely to back Harivansh Narayan Singh for re-election after his previous term ended on April 9. Opposition parties including Congress, TMC and Left have criticised the move as rushed, questioning why the government is prioritising this post while the Lok Sabha Deputy Speaker position remains vacant since 2019.

Opposition parties including Congress, TMC and Left have criticised the move as rushed, questioning why the government is prioritising this post while the Lok Sabha Deputy Speaker position remains vacant since 2019.

articles.summary: Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal stated that delimitation will not affect southern states, as there will be a proportional increase in seats nationwide. He also defended the National Education Policy, criticizing Tamil Nadu's DMK government for failing to realize the state's potential under the policy.

articles.title: The Strait of Hormuz: A Recurrent Flashpoint in Global Power Rivalries
articles.summary: The Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane for global oil trade, remains a persistent flashpoint in international power rivalries. The article highlights the geopolitical tensions in the region, which could disrupt energy supplies and impact India's oil imports. The strait is a key transit route for crude oil, making its stability crucial for India's energy security and economic interests.

articles.title: HyFarm, Sri BioAesthetics in pact to boost potato ecosystem health
articles.summary: HyFarm and Sri BioAesthetics have partnered to address critical issues in India's potato ecosystem, including soil-borne diseases, inconsistent yields, and high rejection rates in processing lines. The collaboration aims to improve seed potato production and processing-grade consistency. These challenges are significant for India's agricultural sector, where potato is a key crop.
articles.raw_summary: This collaboration will address some critical challenges in seed potato production and processing-grade consistency which includes soil-borne diseases, yield variability and high rejection rates in processing lines

articles.summary: Shaktikanta Das, principal secretary to prime minister Modi, highlighted India's economic resilience amidst global uncertainties, likening challenges to a Chakravyuh. He noted the importance of calibrated exits from crises and maintaining low inflation for stability.

articles.title: How India's digital personal data protection law shapes enterprise AI futures
articles.summary: When it comes to cyber disruption and attacks, it’s not a matter of “if,” but “when.” Attackers increasingly target enterprise data rather than infrastructure because data is often the most valuable digital asset an organisation holds, notes Rubrik Inc's Co-founder and CTO, Arvind Nithrakashyap.

articles.title: India mulls payment lags, checks for senior citizens as digital fraud rises, RBI paper shows
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is proposing new rules to combat rising online fraud. High-value digital payments may face a one-hour delay. Extra checks are planned for transactions by citizens aged 70 and above. These measures aim to protect customers from sophisticated scams and financial losses. The central bank is seeking public feedback on these proposals.

articles.title: West Bengal Polls 2026: TMC says EC chief asked delegation to 'get lost', ECI shares ultimatum
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) alleged that Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar told their delegation to "get lost" during discussions on voter deletions in West Bengal. The Election Commission of India (ECI) denied any bias and asserted that the state polls will be conducted fairly. The TMC's claim highlights concerns over electoral processes ahead of the 2026 West Bengal elections. The ECI has not yet responded to the allegations beyond denying them.
articles.raw_summary: TMC alleges Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar told its delegation to "get lost" during talks on voter deletions in West Bengal, EC denies bias and vows free and fair state polls.

articles.title: ‘Apology would set terrible precedent’: Kunal Kamra tells Maharashtra council panel
articles.summary: Comedian Kunal Kamra appeared before a Maharashtra council panel investigating his social media posts. The panel suggested that an unconditional apology could change how they view the matter. Kamra refused, arguing that an apology would set a bad precedent. The interaction highlights ongoing debates about free speech and government oversight of online content.
articles.raw_summary: This came after the committee told the comedian that it would ‘look at the matter differently’ if he were to express unconditional remorse.

articles.title: India to push for reporting of offshore rupee trades despite resistance, says report
articles.summary: India plans to push ahead with a proposal requiring banks to report offshore rupee derivative trades, aiming to improve transparency and ease pressure on the currency. The RBI has proposed that lenders share data on at least 70% of such transactions from February 2027, despite resistance from foreign banks over regulatory concerns.
articles.raw_summary: India plans to push ahead with a proposal requiring banks to report offshore rupee derivative trades, aiming to improve transparency and ease pressure on the currency. The RBI has proposed that lenders share data on at least 70% of such transactions from February 2027, despite resistance from foreign banks over regulatory concerns.

articles.title: India withdraws bid to host COP33 climate summit in 2028
articles.summary: India has withdrawn its bid to host the UN’s COP33 climate summit in 2028, reversing an offer made by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at COP28 in Dubai. The government has not cited a reason.
articles.raw_summary: India has withdrawn its bid to host the UN’s COP33 climate summit in 2028, reversing an offer made by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at COP28 in Dubai. The government has not cited a reason.

articles.title: Big Tech puts financial heft behind next-gen nuclear power as AI demand surges
articles.summary: Several US firms are investing in and developing smaller, modular nuclear reactors that are more advanced and scalable than traditional plants. This shift is driven by rising demand for energy, particularly for powering AI data centers. The development could impact global energy policies, including India's, as the country seeks to diversify its energy mix with cleaner and more efficient nuclear options.

articles.title: Can Criminal Proceedings Be Quashed If Evidence Disproves Allegations? Supreme Court Clarifies
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has clarified the circumstances under which criminal proceedings can be quashed if evidence disproves allegations. The ruling reinforces limits on the misuse of criminal law. This decision impacts how courts handle cases where evidence contradicts initial accusations, ensuring fairer legal processes. The judgment is significant for preventing abuse of the criminal justice system.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court clarifies when criminal proceedings can be quashed if evidence disproves allegations, reinforcing limits of misuse of criminal law.

articles.title: Andhra Pradesh plans law to regulate social media access for children under 13
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government is planning to introduce a law to regulate social media access for children under 13. The proposed law includes exploring age verification mechanisms, tightening action against online abuse, and promoting digital safety education in schools. This initiative aims to protect children from potential harms associated with social media use. The state is considering these measures as part of broader efforts to enhance child welfare and digital governance.
articles.raw_summary: Andhra Pradesh plans law to restrict social media for children under 13, explore age verification, tighten action on online abuse, and promote digital safety education in schools

articles.title: Government raises gas supply to fertiliser plants, boosts fuel allocation amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has raised gas supply to fertilizer plants by 5%, reaching around 95% of their six-month average consumption starting April 9, 2026. This decision is based on available inventory and scheduled LNG cargo arrivals amid tensions in West Asia. The move aims to ensure adequate fuel supply for fertilizer production, which is critical for India's agricultural sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has increased gas allocation to fertilizer plants by 5% to around 95% of their six-month average consumption, effective April 9, 2026, based on available inventory and scheduled LNG cargo arrivals.

articles.title: Cashfree appoints ex-Visa finance head Sameer Gandhi as its new CFO
articles.summary: Bengaluru payments processor Cashfree has appointed Sameer Gandhi as its new Chief Financial Officer. Gandhi previously led finance at Visa India. He will now guide Cashfree's financial strategy and enhance commercial operations. This move supports the company's aim for accelerated growth and profitability in the coming quarters. Cashfree processes over $80 billion in transactions annually.

Cashfree processes over $80 billion in transactions annually.

articles.title: Unnat Krishi Mahotsav concludes, farmers to emerge as energy, fuel & hydrogen providers, says Gadkari
articles.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari concluded the Unnat Krishi Mahotsav, highlighting how agricultural residues, biomass, ethanol, compressed bio-gas, and hydrogen can create new income opportunities for farmers. He emphasized the untapped potential of these resources in transforming farmers into energy and fuel providers. The event underscores the government's push for sustainable agricultural practices and energy diversification.
articles.raw_summary: Nithin Gatkari said that agricultural residues, biomass, ethanol, compressed bio-gas, and hydrogen present untapped income opportunities for farmers

articles.title: Person born in India has right to remain on electoral roll, vote: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court observed that a person born in India has a constitutional and sentimental right to be on the electoral roll and vote. The bench indicated it would re-examine election results if victory margins are low and voter deletions are high, particularly in cases involving scrutiny of older voter lists.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court observed that a person born in India has a constitutional and sentimental right to be on the electoral roll and vote. The bench indicated it would re-examine election results if victory margins are low and voter deletions are high, particularly in cases involving scrutiny of older voter lists.

articles.title: 'Don't forget you work in BJP states': Suvendu Adhikari's warning in Bengal
articles.summary: Opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari warned Muslim migrant workers in Nandigram that they might lose work in BJP-governed states after the assembly polls. He cited the presence of thousands of workers in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Odisha, all ruled by the BJP. The TMC criticized Adhikari's remarks as threats and intimidation, vowing to report the matter to the Election Commission.
articles.raw_summary: Opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari warned Muslim migrant workers in Nandigram that they might lose work in BJP-governed states after the assembly polls. He cited the presence of thousands of workers in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Odisha, all ruled by the BJP. The TMC criticized Adhikari's remarks as threats and intimidation, vowing to report the matter to the Election Commission.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: 77-yr-old dies of 'heart attack' as kin fight home voting officials
articles.summary: In North Dinajpur, West Bengal, a 77-year-old woman died during a dispute over voting procedures for a blind voter. Family members of the disabled voter clashed with election officials, demanding the presence of the voter's daughter, leading to a commotion where the woman collapsed. Doctors suspect a heart attack. The incident raises questions about election accessibility and disability rights in India's polling process.
articles.raw_summary: A tragic incident unfolded in North Dinajpur as an elderly woman died during a dispute over voting procedures. Family members of a blind voter clashed with election officials, demanding a disabled daughter's presence. The ensuing commotion led to the woman's collapse, with doctors suspecting a heart attack.

articles.title: Change of guard: Pirojsha to take over from Nadir Godrej
articles.summary: Nadir Godrej will step down as Godrej Industries Group chair on August 13. His nephew, Pirojsha Godrej, will assume the role of GIG chair. Pirojsha will also chair Godrej Industries, the group's holding company. Nadir's son, Burjis Godrej, will become chair of Godrej Agrovet. This marks a significant generational leadership change within the prominent Indian conglomerate.
articles.raw_summary: Nadir Godrej will step down as Godrej Industries Group chair on August 13. His nephew, Pirojsha Godrej, will assume the role of GIG chair. Pirojsha will also chair Godrej Industries, the group's holding company. Nadir's son, Burjis Godrej, will become chair of Godrej Agrovet. This marks a significant generational leadership change within the prominent Indian conglomerate.

articles.title: Advait Energy emerges lowest bidder for DGVCL turnkey power project tender
articles.summary: Advait Energy has been selected as the lowest bidder for a turnkey power project tender issued by DGVCL. The project involves supplying and installing 11KV medium voltage covered conductors under the Vanbandhu Kalyan Yojana-2 (VKY-2) scheme, aimed at tribal welfare. This is part of a government-backed initiative to improve power infrastructure in tribal areas.
articles.raw_summary: The tender pertains to the supply, installation, testing and commissioning of 11KV 55mm² AAAC Medium Voltage Covered Conductor (MVCC), along with accessories and additional poles if required, under the Vanbandhu Kalyan Yojana-2 (VKY-2) scheme or other schemes.

articles.title: Wage hike, weekly off among key demands as late-night talks held in Noida after massive protest
articles.summary: A massive protest in Noida led to late-night talks after workers demanded a wage hike and weekly off. The Uttar Pradesh government formed a committee to examine these demands following a directive by Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath. The situation highlights labour welfare issues and may prompt policy action.
articles.raw_summary: The committee by the UP government was constituted on Monday, following a directive by CM Yogi Adityanath, to address the situation, examine demands.

articles.title: DMRC begins mid-life refurbishment of Blue Line trains, 70 trains to be upgraded in phases
articles.summary: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) has begun refurbishing 70 trains across the Red and Blue Lines in phases. In the first two phases, 30 trains have already been upgraded, with work on another 18 trains set to start soon. The third phase will cover 22 Blue Line trains by November 2027. This initiative aims to modernize and extend the lifespan of the metro fleet, improving public transportation infrastructure in Delhi.
articles.raw_summary: DMRC is refurbishing 70 trains across the Red and Blue Lines in a phased manner. In the first phase, 12 trains have already been upgraded, while 18 trains were refurbished in the second phase. Work on another nine trains is set to begin soon, followed by an additional nine trains. In the third phase, 22 Blue Line trains will be refurbished by November 2027.

The U.S. and Iran are the key parties involved in the dialogue.

Brent and WTI crude futures saw declines. Analysts suggest tight oil balances could keep prices elevated. International bodies urged nations to avoid hoarding energy supplies.

articles.title: China’s export growth slowed sharply in first month of Iran War
articles.summary: China's export growth slowed sharply to 2.5% in March due to disruptions in energy supplies caused by the Iran war. The conflict's impact on global energy markets may have broader implications for international trade and energy policies.

articles.title: From diversity to monotony, ecological communities are homogenising
articles.summary: The article explores biotic homogenisation, where diverse ecosystems worldwide are losing unique species and becoming increasingly similar due to environmental changes. This process replaces sensitive endemic species with common generalist ones, raising concerns about ecological balance and biodiversity loss. The phenomenon is documented globally but has implications for India's environmental policies and conservation strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Imagine walking through different ecosystems across the world, only to find that species inhabiting them look strikingly similar. This is the reality of biotic homogenisation, a process where diverse biological communities become increasingly similar as unique endemic species, highly sensitive to environmental changes, are replaced by common generalist species, which are insensitive to such perturbations. [ ]

articles.summary: According to a study by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII), dolphins require a minimum depth of three metres in flowing rivers to survive during lean seasons. articles.title: Net FDI inflows dip, investors opting for Mexico, Vietnam
articles.summary: India's net foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows have declined as investors increasingly favor Mexico, Poland, and Vietnam for nearshoring and friendshoring strategies after the Covid pandemic. Experts attribute this shift to these countries' competitive advantages and the US securing a large share of tech-related investments. The trend raises concerns about India's attractiveness as an investment destination compared to its peers.
articles.raw_summary: Officials and experts attribute this trend to the country having to compete with Mexico, Poland, and Vietnam, a preferred part of investors' nearshoring and friendshoring policies after the Covid pandemic, and the US cornering a large share of tech-related investments.

articles.summary: Anthropic’s new AI model, Mythos, has prompted rare, coordinated alerts from major central banks worldwide, including the U.S. Federal Reserve and European Central Bank, warning of potential cyber risks posed by advanced algorithms. The model’s capabilities have raised concerns about systemic vulnerabilities in financial systems, as AI-driven threats could emerge as a new frontier for systemic risk alongside traditional market risks. articles.raw_summary: Anthoripic Mythos: A single AI model has prompted rare, near-synchronized action by major central banks, signalling that the next systemic risk to finance may emerge from algorithms as much as markets.

articles.title: IAF ties up with GE to build own facility for repair, overhaul of Tejas engines with quicker turnaround
articles.summary: The Indian Air Force (IAF) will establish and operate its own depot facility for repairing and overhauling Tejas jet engines, with technical support from GE Aerospace. The facility aims to reduce turnaround time for engine maintenance. GE will provide training, spare parts, and specialized equipment, while the IAF will own and manage the facility. This move aligns with India's push for indigenous defence capabilities and reduced reliance on foreign maintenance support.

articles.summary: The Punjab and Haryana High Court recently observed that a husband has a legal duty to maintain his wife and he can't be absolved of this responsibility merely on the ground that he is a student. " (husband) cannot be permitted to plead that he is unable to maintain his wife due to financial constraints as long as he is capable of earning. articles.title: Top updates: Trump says US will blockade Strait of Hormuz as Iran talks fail
articles.summary: The US has threatened to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations with Iran collapsed, failing to end the conflict in West Asia. The Strait is a critical global oil transit route, and any disruption could impact fuel prices and energy security worldwide. The development follows Washington's failed attempts to reach an agreement with Tehran, raising regional tensions.

articles.title: Four cheetah cubs born in the wild in Kuno for first time since launch of translocation project
articles.summary: Four cheetah cubs have been born in the wild in Kuno National Park for the first time since India launched its cheetah translocation project. All previous births had occurred in monitored enclosures. The project, involving government agencies, aims to reintroduce cheetahs to India after their local extinction decades ago. This development marks a significant milestone in wildlife conservation efforts.

articles.title: Man can’t be charged with kidnapping if minor voluntarily elopes with him, says Chhattisgarh HC
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court acquitted a 24-year-old man who had been sentenced to 20 years in prison under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act. The court ruled that a minor voluntarily eloping with an adult does not constitute kidnapping under the law. The decision sets a legal precedent regarding the interpretation of POCSO and child rights in cases of consensual relationships involving minors.

articles.summary: India's retail inflation rose to 3.40% in March from 3.21% in February, remaining below the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) acceptable range of 2-6%. The data provides context for potential policy responses by the central bank or government to manage price stability.

articles.title: Recyclable Materials Mean Nothing if the Product Cannot Be Disassembled
articles.summary: Performance outerwear, due to its complex multi-material construction and bonded components, is nearly impossible to recycle. A new research project is testing whether redesigning products for easier disassembly could solve this challenge. The apparel industry's technical complexity has long made end-of-life recovery difficult, but this initiative aims to change that from the design stage. The outcome could inform future policies on sustainable fashion and textile waste management.
articles.raw_summary: Performance outerwear is among the most technically complex categories in apparel—and among the hardest to recycle. Multi-material construction, bonded membranes, and inseparable trims have long made end-of-life recovery a structural impossibility rather than a logistical inconvenience. A new research and development project is testing whether design for disassembly can change that calculus from the ground up.

This could lower rice output in the region, which is a major global supplier. The crisis may lead to higher food prices and supply chain disruptions, potentially affecting India's food security and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: War-driven surge in fuel and fertilizer costs forces farmers across Southeast Asia to delay harvests, scale back sowing and risk lower output.

articles.title: West Asia conflict triggers cost, logistics pressures in India’s castings sector
articles.summary: Kolkata, Apr 10 (PTI) The Iran-US conflict has triggered significant cost pressures and logistics disruptions for India’s foundry and castings sector, with pig iron prices surging over 13 per cent in three months and freight rates to key markets spiking by up to 60 per cent, according to stakeholders. articles.summary: The Supreme Court agreed to examine whether biometric and facial recognition technology can be used for voter authentication at polling booths. The bench noted that implementing such a system would not be a simple administrative task. The court's decision could have significant implications for India's electoral processes and data privacy frameworks.

articles.title: West Asia conflict: Govt to setup weekly monitoring mechanism to track exim trends
articles.summary: The Indian government is setting up a weekly system to monitor export-import trends and industry stress, aiming to address supply chain disruptions and rising costs affecting sectors like apparel and medical devices. Customs procedures for hazardous cargo will also be streamlined. The initiative seeks to improve transparency and efficiency in shipping and logistics, ensuring smoother trade operations.

The initiative aims to boost transparency and efficiency in shipping and logistics, ensuring smoother trade operations.

articles.summary: Telangana will conduct the first phase of the digital Census 2027 from May 11 to June 9, followed by a second phase in February 2027. articles.title: Iran US War Live Updates: Tehran slams US blockade as 'grave violation of sovereignty'
articles.summary: High-level US and Iranian officials held talks in Islamabad but did not reach a breakthrough. Discussions continue on key issues like the Strait of Hormuz (a vital oil shipping route), nuclear concerns, and sanctions. Iran criticized the US blockade as a 'grave violation of sovereignty.' The outcome could affect regional stability and global oil markets, which may have implications for India's energy imports and diplomatic stance.

articles.title: SC refuses to entertain plea against deletion of names from WB voter list in SIR, terms it 'premature'
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea against the deletion of names from the West Bengal voter list, calling the petition premature. The plea alleged that the Election Commission was deleting names without following due process and that appeals against these deletions were not being heard in a timely manner. The court's decision highlights concerns over the Election Commission's procedures for voter list management.
articles.raw_summary: The plea alleged that the Election Commission was summarily deleting names without following due process, and that appeals against these deletions were not being heard in a timely manner.

articles.title: Scott Bessent, Jerome Powell warn bank CEOs about Anthropic Mythos risks
articles.summary: US officials, including Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and Scott Bessent, warned major bank CEOs about risks posed by Anthropic's AI model Mythos. The model is reported to identify and exploit flaws in critical systems, with access restricted to select tech firms. The warning underscores concerns about AI-driven cybersecurity threats that could impact financial and technological infrastructure globally, including India.
articles.raw_summary: US officials warned major bank CEOs that Anthropic AI model Mythos poses serious cyber risks, as it can find and exploit flaws in major systems, access is limited to select tech firms

articles.title: TCS pledges strict action on Nashik harassment case; COO Subramanian leads investigation
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has appointed its COO Aarthi Subramanian to investigate a sexual harassment case at its Nashik unit. Eight employees alleged mental and sexual harassment by senior colleagues, with claims of HR inaction. Seven individuals, including an HR manager, have been arrested by police. The incident raises concerns about how IT companies manage operations in smaller cities and the adequacy of workplace safeguards.

The southwest monsoon is vital for irrigating half of India's farmland. A deficit season could impact crop yields, affecting food prices and economic growth.

The Strait of Hormuz is a key chokepoint for global oil transit, and any disruption could have significant economic and energy security implications for countries like India that rely heavily on oil imports.

articles.title: World Bank: South Asian labour market pivoting towards skills that can be amplified by AI
articles.summary: The World Bank's Chief Economist Franziska Ohnsorge noted that hiring has slowed in occupations exposed to AI, with job prospects eroding due to long-standing labour market disparities. This trend is part of a broader shift in South Asian labour markets toward skills that can be enhanced by AI. The findings suggest potential challenges for workforce adaptation and policy interventions in regions like India.

The situation highlights potential risks to energy markets and supply chains, which could impact India's energy imports and pricing.
